_comment: QuantaFoundry app-level cross-model intents. Author the FULL application unitary U[out,in] (2^n_sys square, complex) from the application's standard definition — big-endian (first register = MSB; control = first). Same conventions as the gate round. These are application *intents* (app_golden); the decomposition into sealed modules is QuantaFoundry's job. See APP-GOLDEN-BRIEF.md.

convention.controlled_gates: first register = control, last register = target

intents.name: Bell-state preparation
intents.definition: Two-qubit Bell preparation unitary U = CNOT*(H tensor I): apply Hadamard to the first qubit, then CNOT (control=first, target=second). It maps |00> -> (|00>+|11>)/sqrt(2).

intents.name: GHZ-3 preparation
intents.definition: Three-qubit GHZ preparation unitary: Hadamard on qubit 0, then CNOT(0->1), then CNOT(1->2). Maps |000> -> (|000>+|111>)/sqrt(2).

intents.name: GHZ-4 preparation
intents.definition: Four-qubit GHZ preparation unitary: Hadamard on qubit 0, then CNOT(0->1), CNOT(1->2), CNOT(2->3). Maps |0000> -> (|0000>+|1111>)/sqrt(2).

intents.name: Reflection about |00>
intents.definition: Two-qubit reflection operator 2|00><00| - I: +1 on |00>, -1 on the other three basis states. (Global phase is free, so diag(1,-1,-1,-1) and diag(-1,1,1,1) are equivalent.)

intents.name: Grover diffusion (2 qubits)
intents.definition: Two-qubit Grover diffusion operator D = (H tensor H) * (2|00><00| - I) * (H tensor H).

intents.name: Grover iterate (2 qubits, mark |11>)
intents.definition: Two-qubit Grover single iterate G = D * O, where O = diag(1,1,1,-1) marks |11> and D is the Grover diffusion operator. Applied to the uniform superposition it produces |11>.

intents.name: QFT-2 (pipeline target)
intents.definition: Quantum Fourier Transform on 2 qubits. U[j,k] = (1/sqrt(N)) * exp(2*pi*i*j*k/N), N=4, big-endian integer indexing, NO output bit-reversal (raw QFT matrix). This is the intent of the QFT-2 pipeline decomposition (H + controlled-phase + swap).

intents.name: QFT-3 (pipeline target)
intents.definition: Quantum Fourier Transform on 3 qubits. U[j,k] = (1/sqrt(N)) * exp(2*pi*i*j*k/N), N=8, big-endian integer indexing, NO output bit-reversal (raw QFT matrix). Intent of the QFT-3 pipeline.
